name: Chocolate Toffee
recipeInstructions:
1. COMBINE the sugar, corn syrup, and half a cup of cream in a large saucepan set over moderate heat.
2. STIR the mixture until the sugar dissolves completely.
3. INSERT a candy thermometer into the mixture. REDUCE the heat to low and COOK the syrup, stirring frequently, until the thermometer registers 238°F.
4. BLEND in another half cup of cream, which will cause the temperature to drop. CONTINUE to cook and STIR until the thermometer reaches 236°F or until a bit of the hot toffee dropped into cold water forms a soft, pliable ball.
5. MIX in the remaining half cup of cream and the melted chocolate.
6. COOK the toffee, stirring CONSTANTLY, lest it scorch.
7. CONTINUE to COOK the toffee until the mixture becomes QUITE THICK: A drop of it should firm up quickly when placed in cold water.
8. QUICKLY MIX in vanilla and salt.
9. POUR the toffee into a well-buttered 8-by-8-by-2-inch pan.
10. ALLOW the toffee to COOL COMPLETELY.
11. CUT the cooled toffee into 1-inch squares.
12. WRAP each square in waxed paper or plastic wrap.
